channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Higashioka's homer starts rally as Padres beat Braves 5-4 to sweep NL Wild Card Series
item.description: SAN DIEGO (AP) — Kyle Higashioka's solo homer started a five-run rally against an ailing Max Fried with two outs in the second inning, and the San Diego Padres held on to sweep the Atlanta Braves with a 5-4 win in Game 2 of their NL Wild Card Series on Wednesday night.
item.dc:creator: BERNIE WILSON, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Orioles get swept out of the playoffs in their only series for a second consecutive year
item.description: BALTIMORE (AP) — Things went so poorly for the Baltimore Orioles in the playoffs that even when they had the bases loaded and a hit by pitch scores a run, Colton Cowser swung at a ball thrown right at him, missed and broke his left hand in the process.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN WHYNO, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Lionel Messi, Inter Miami win MLS Supporters Shield with 3-2 win at Crew. It's Messi's 46th trophy.
item.description: Lionel Messi and Inter Miami have another trophy, along with home-field advantage throughout the Major League Soccer playoffs.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Bobby Witt Jr. and the Royals go from 2 late 7-game losing streaks to the AL Division Series
item.description: BALTIMORE (AP) — After another September loss gave the Kansas City Royals a second seven-game slide in the final month of the season, Will Smith saw the upcoming off day as a chance to make his teammates laugh. “You guys are looking at this the wrong way,” Smith said.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN WHYNO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Bills place emphasis on defensive backups in wake of NFL's 4-game suspension of Von Miller
item.description: ORCHARD PARK, N.Y. (AP) — Bills cornerback Rasul Douglas didn’t think anything of Von Miller's absence at practice on Wednesday, assuming the edge rusher had a rest day, as sometimes happens for the 35-year-old veteran.
item.dc:creator: JOHN WAWROW, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Californians think this is the unofficial boundary between Northern and Southern California
item.description: There is no true defining line that represents the distinction between Northern and Southern California, but it’s still a widely popular debate. Some Californians believe the transition from SoCal to NorCal happens when sports fans cheer for the Giants and 49ers as opposed to the Dodgers and Rams.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Bucs remain banged up but carry confidence into Thursday's Falcons clash
item.description: The Tampa Bay Buccaneers are riding momentum into Thursday night’s NFC South clash with the Atlanta Falcons but so far, the short game week has presented many challenges.
item.dc:creator: Dan Lucas, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Some Caitlin Clark fans are pretenders, 'pushing racist agendas,' WNBA legend Sue Bird says
item.description: WNBA legend Sue Bird spoke out against a large portion of Caitlin Clark's fanbase on Wednesday, claiming they are only invested in the player to push a racist agenda.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Aggies face another tough challenge at #21 Boise State
item.description: LOGAN, Utah (ABC4 Sports) - The Aggies have had two weeks to stew in that second half collapse at Temple when they got outscored in the second half 28-8 to fall to 1-3 on the season. "Obviously, our record is not what we want," said quarterback Spencer Petras on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Pete Rose speaks with KTLA 5 in final interview
item.description: Pete Rose, Major League Baseball's all-time leader in hits and one of baseball's most divisive figures, died Monday at his home in Las Vegas. He was 83.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, KTLA 5

item.title: WWE NXT heads to Chicago for live debut on CW Network
item.description: The event marks a significant moment for WWE NXT as it expands its reach to a broader audience on the CW Network.
item.dc:creator: Kenny Morales, NewsNation

item.title: MLB regular season ends Sunday, playoffs to begin Oct. 1
item.description: The MLB post-season begins Oct. 1 with the Wild Card series. The end of the regular season marks an end to the Athletics' time in Oakland.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Soriano, NewsNation

item.title: Utah Royals beat Racing Lousiville 1-0
item.description: In their third-to-last home game of the season, Utah Royals FC beat Racing Louisville 1-0 off of a goal from Ana Tejada. There were chances from both sides during the match, but only one shot made it into the back of the net on the night.
item.dc:creator: Stockton Mair, ABC 4

item.title: Kyle Hendricks sharp in what may have been Cubs' finale, 3-0 win over Reds
item.description: Kyle Hendricks waved to appreciative fans after what may have been his final start for the Cubs, and Chicago beat the Cincinnati Reds 3-0 on Saturday as Isaac Paredes and Pete Crow-Armstrong had run-scoring singles in a three-run eighth inning.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: USF football falls at Tulane in conference opener
item.description: TAMPA, Fla. (USF) -- The South Florida football team fell in its conference-opening clash with Tulane, 45-10, at Yulman Stadium in New Orleans Saturday afternoon.
item.dc:creator: University of South Florida, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Jazz management excited about youth movement
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - Back in April, Jazz CEO of Basketball Operations Danny Ainge had his sights on acquiring a big-name player. "Our objective is to find a player or two, and we're we're ready to roll," Ainge said five months ago. "We're ready to go big game hunting.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Tom Brady helps Big Ten kickoff nationwide blood drive
item.description: The Big Ten and Abbott have launched a nation-wide blood drive competition to tackle the generational shortage of blood, with the university with the most donations receiving one-million dollars, as the U.S. is currently experiencing one of the worst blood shortages in a generation.
item.dc:creator: Dina Bair, WGN 9

item.title: RSL pushes for home playoff advantage in crucial showdown with Austin FC
item.description: Having clinched their playoff spot last weekend, RSL wants a top-four finish and the accompanying home-field advantage in the first round.
item.dc:creator: Mattey Casey, Royal Riot Podcast, ABC 4
